input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule F.10.2.3 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.10.2.3
            TITLE: the accepted methods of joining walls to walls and walls to floor are:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.10.2.3: The accepted methods of joining walls to walls and walls to floor are:
F.10.2.3 The accepted methods of joining walls to walls and walls to floor are: 
a. Welding 
• Welds may be continuous or interrupted.  
• If interrupted, the weld/space ratio must be 1:1 or higher  
• All weld lengths must be more than 25 mm 
b. Fasteners  
Combined strength of the fasteners must be Equivalent to the strength of the welded 
joint ( F.10.2.3.a above ) 
c. Bonding 
• Bonding must meet  F.5.5 
• Strength of the bonded joint must be Equivalent to the strength of the welded joint 
( F.10.2.3.a above ) 
• Bonds must run the entire length of the joint

final_answer:
The accepted methods of joining walls to walls and walls to floor are:
a. Welding
• Welds may be continuous or interrupted.
• If interrupted, the weld/space ratio must be 1:1 or higher
• All weld lengths must be more than 25 mm
b. Fasteners
Combined strength of the fasteners must be Equivalent to the strength of the welded joint ( F.10.2.3.a above )
c. Bonding
• Bonding must meet F.5.5
Strength of the bonded joint must be Equivalent to the strength of the welded joint ( F.10.2.3.a above )
• Bonds must run the entire length of the joint
